title: anagallis monelli var. maritima
intro: Anagallis monelli var. maritima, also known as seaside or sea pimpernel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is native to coastal areas in Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or ointment to accelerate wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
